matcher.subject: Mathematics
matcher.coveragePreview: number system - session 1 grade 9 students classification of real numbers decimal expansions geometric representation of irrationals the number system: foundations introduction to real numbers subject: mathematics chapter: number system session 1 of 5 duration: 45 minutes what we will learn today number classification decimal types geometric representation classify numbers into natural, whole, integer, rational, and real. identify terminating vs. non-terminating recurring decimals. construct irrational numbers geometrically on the number line. recall: the number hierarchy natural numbers whole numbers integers natural numbers: 1, 2, 3... whole numbers: 0, 1, 2... integers: ..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2... can you measure a circle exactly? rational vs irrational decimal expansions pi (π) ≈ 3.14159... does it ever end? can we write it as a fraction? the world of rational numbers definition of rational numbers decimal expansions definition: p/q where q ≠ 0. terminating decimals (e.g., 0.5). non-terminating recurring (e.g., 0.333...). the non-repeating mystery irrational numbers real number system cannot be written as p/q. non-terminating & non-recurring.
